title: ARPA-H PROSPR Program Awards $144M to Seven Teams Targeting Aging
description: ARPA-H announced seven research teams funded through its PROSPR program, investing up to $144M to develop tools and therapies for extending healthspan, including the first Phase 3 clinical trials targeting aging with rapamycin, SGLT2 inhibitors, and semaglutide.

title: First Phase 3 Clinical Trial in Aging: Rapamycin, SGLT2i, and GLP-1 Selected
description: UT Health San Antonio will launch the VITAL-H Trial backed by $38M in ARPA-H funding, testing rapamycin, dapagliflozin, and semaglutide in healthy adults aged 60-65 in the first nationwide Phase 3 study focused on healthy longevity.

title: Life Biosciences Clears FDA for First Human Cellular Reprogramming Trial
description: Life Biosciences received FDA IND clearance for ER-100, the first-ever human trial of partial epigenetic reprogramming technology, targeting vision loss from glaucoma and NAION. Patient enrollment began in March.

title: $22M Federal Award to Study HIV Drug Censavudine for Slowing Aging
description: Brown University and University of Rochester will lead a clinical trial testing whether Censavudine, an HIV drug, can suppress retrotransposon activity and reduce biological aging in 200 healthy adults aged 60-65 over 48 weeks.

title: Columbia University Joins ARPA-H PROSPR to Identify Aging Biomarkers
description: Columbia's FAST project will analyze data from completed clinical trials of metformin, SGLT-2 inhibitors, GLP-1 agonists, and rapamycin to discover novel biomarkers revealing how these drugs affect aging biology.

title: Fat Composition Found to Affect T Cell-Mediated Immunity in Aging
description: Scientists discovered that the ratio between poly- and monounsaturated fatty acids affects T cell viability and both humoral and cellular immunity, with implications for aging immune function.

title: More Confirming Data for Adult Human Neurogenesis
description: New evidence confirms that new neurons are generated in the adult human brain and integrate into existing neural networks, resolving a long-standing debate in neuroscience with implications for aging and cognitive decline.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: Senolytic Effectiveness and NK Cell Restoration
description: Monthly roundup highlights research on mitochondrial stress boosting senolytic effectiveness (Nature Aging) and restoring aged natural killer cells' ability to eliminate harmful cells (Aging Cell).

title: Insilico Medicine and Liquid AI Launch Secure AI for Drug Research
description: Insilico Medicine partnered with Liquid AI to launch a secure, lightweight AI platform designed to streamline drug research and development in the longevity space.

title: GMP Production Begins for Scalable Anti-Aging 'Longevity Protein' Therapy
description: Production has begun under GMP standards to support a scalable anti-aging therapy built around the so-called longevity protein, marking a key manufacturing milestone for translational longevity medicine.

title: Circadian Disruption Linked to Inflammatory Microglia in Aging Brain
description: Animal research connects circadian disruption with inflammatory microglia, while new funding explores extracellular vesicle therapy for age-related neurodegeneration.

title: New AI Platform Combines Blood Biomarkers and Lifestyle Data for Personalized Aging Insights
description: A new platform combines blood biomarkers, lifestyle data, and AI to give people personalized insights on biological aging and chronic disease risk.

title: Longevity Innovation Forum Launching in San Diego, March 11-12
description: Longevity Global will host the inaugural Longevity Innovation Forum in San Diego, bringing together scientists, clinicians, biotech founders, and investors advancing healthy aging research.

title: Novel Mechanism for Parkinson's Linked to ATP Deficiency
description: ATP deficiency disrupts dopamine processing in synapses, leading to accumulation of harmful protein species that characterize Parkinson's disease, revealing a new therapeutic target.

title: Microbiome Depletion Rejuvenates the Aging Brain
description: New research published in the Fight Aging! archives shows that microbiome depletion can rejuvenate the aging brain, suggesting gut-brain axis interventions as a potential anti-aging strategy.

title: Longevity Biotech Stocks Surge as $27 Trillion Healthcare Shift Favors Cell Restoration
description: Global capital is rotating into 'Longevity Infrastructure' valued at $27T by 2030, with cell therapy markets hitting $8.85B by 2026 and AI-driven drug discovery accelerating to $24.51B.

title: Longevity Market: 81 Funding Deals Tracked in Comprehensive Database
description: A comprehensive database tracking 81 longevity funding deals highlights Retro Biosciences' $1B raise in Q1 2025 as the largest single round since Altos Labs' $3B launch.

title: David Sinclair at World Government Summit: 'Ageing Could Soon Be Reversible'
description: Harvard scientist David Sinclair presented at WGS 2026, explaining that aging is driven by chemical changes in DNA rather than irreversible damage, with reversal now within scientific reach.

title: Longevity Investing in 2026: Strategic Guide for Family Offices
description: The longevity biotech market is valued at $30.7B in 2026, projected to reach $52.8B by 2035 at 6.5% CAGR, with epigenetic reprogramming, senolytics, and AI-driven drug discovery driving growth.

title: Rhonda Patrick: Diet Directly Shapes Sleep Quality Through Fiber and Plant Diversity
description: Dr. Rhonda Patrick shared research showing higher-fiber, plant-diverse days produce more deep and REM sleep with lower overnight heart rate, while processed foods worsen sleep quality.

title: ERG Phase Separation Attenuates Cellular Senescence
description: New research explores how ERG phase separation mechanisms can attenuate cellular senescence, offering potential new therapeutic targets for age-related disease.

title: AI-Driven Senolytic Predictor Discovers Novel Compounds Including Panaxatriol
description: Machine learning-based senolytic predictor identified panaxatriol and voclosporin as potential anti-aging compounds, with voclosporin extending C. elegans lifespan more effectively than metformin.

title: Stanford MGR Features Valter Longo on Fasting-Mimicking Diets and Longevity
description: Stanford Medicine hosts USC gerontology professor Valter Longo on March 11 to discuss fasting-mimicking diets and longevity research for transforming healthy aging and disease prevention.

title: Rhonda Patrick: UPF Consumption in Early Life Linked to Worse Behavioral Outcomes
description: Dr. Rhonda Patrick highlighted research showing children consuming more ultra-processed foods at age 3 had higher anxiety, depression, and aggression by age 5, with 10% UPF replacement showing improvements.

title: NewLimit Raises $130M to Push Epigenetic Reprogramming Toward Humans
description: NewLimit secured $130M in funding to advance epigenetic reprogramming technology toward human applications, as the convergence of mitochondria and epigenetics research accelerates.
